data.title: Canaan secures order for Avalon A15 Pro mining machines; shares up over 7%
data.description:
• Canaan (NASDAQ:CAN) shares jumped over 7% during pre-market trading on Thursday after the company said it has secured a purchase order from a U.S.-based bitcoin miner for more than 50,000 of its latest-generation Avalon A15 Pro mining machines.
  • "This milestone order represents a significant win for Canaan and reflects the robust resurgence of the U.S. market," said Nangeng Zhang, chairman and CEO of Canaan. "It highlights not only the strength of our Avalon A15 Pro but also our deep commitment to serving customers worldwide, with a particular focus on building long-term partnerships in the U.S. market. With a focus on delivering machines that combine quality, performance, and strong return on investment, we remain dedicated to delivering the highest quality mining infrastructure and to supporting our customers' growth with innovation, reliability, and trust."
  • CAN +7.69% premarket to $1.12.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ARMOUR Residential REIT, Inc. 7% SER C CUM PFD declares $0.1458 dividend
data.description:
• ARMOUR Residential REIT, Inc. 7% SER C CUM PFD (NYSE:ARR.PR.C) declares $0.1458/share monthly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 8.01%
  • Payable Oct. 27; for shareholders of record Oct. 15; ex-div Oct. 15.
  • Payable Nov. 28; for shareholders of record Nov. 15; ex-div Nov. 15.
  • Payable Dec. 29; for shareholders of record Dec. 15; ex-div Dec. 15.
  • See ARR.PR.C Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: CoreWeave amends credit agreement with US Bank Trust, adds $3B
data.description:
• CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) disclosed on Thursday that it has amended its credit agreement with U.S. Bank Trust, and added an incremental $3B billion tranche of delayed draw term loans.
  • CoreWeave intends to use the money to buy certain equipment, hardware, infrastructure and other systems, the company said in a filing.
  • CoreWeave shares rose 2.3% in premarket trading on Thursday.

data.title: Interlink Electronics secures $200,000 FDA grant
data.description:
• Interlink Electronics (NASDAQ:LINK) announced on Thursday that its Gas and Environmental Solutions division has received a $200,000 SBIR Phase 1 grant from the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
  • The funding will accelerate the development of advanced electrochemical sensors designed to improve the safety and quality monitoring of post-harvest food products, said the company.
  • LINK +2.43% premarket to $14.71.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tesla Q3 deliveries beat estimates, sets quarterly record
data.description:
• Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) delivered 497.1K vehicles in the third quarter, a quarterly record for the company, and surpassing consensus estimates for 448K deliveries as buyers rushed to capitalize on the end of the $7,500 government tax credit.
  • The company produced 447K vehicles during the quarter.
  • By model, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) produced 435.8K of the Model Y and Model 3, delivering 481,166 of the two vehicles.
  • For comparison, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) reported 336,681 vehicle deliveries for Q1 of 2025, 384,122 vehicles in Q2 of 2025, and 462,890 deliveries for Q3 of 2024.

data.title: Bullish sentiment rises slightly as bearish outlook holds steady, shows recent AAII Survey
data.description:
Bullish sentiment among individual investors increased for the week ending October 1, 2025, according to the latest survey from the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII).

Bullish sentiment rose to 42.9%, up from 41.7% the previous week. Meanwhile, bearish sentiment remained steady at 39.2%, unchanged from the prior week. This indicates that while optimism has increased, caution still dominates investor sentiment as concerns about economic challenges linger.

Markets moved through a week of mixed global signals, with the S&P 500 notching a record close as Wall Street rallied despite persistent concerns over a potential U.S. government shutdown. Investor sentiment was supported by strength in Asian and European markets, including a record high in South Korea on a chip rally, while U.S. index futures ticked higher following the early release of job cuts data.

Still, signs of economic fragility persisted. Australia’s trade surplus dropped to a seven-year low, Euro Area factory activity softened, and inflation expectations edged higher. With interest rate uncertainty and global growth concerns still present, many investors remained cautious.

Neutral sentiment, which reflects expectations that stock prices will remain relatively unchanged over the next six months, decreased to 17.9%, down from 19.1% in the prior update. This suggests that a smaller portion of investors are adopting a neutral stance, with most either leaning bullish or bearish.

The survey, which has been conducted by the AAII since 1987, remains a key gauge of individual investor sentiment, offering insights into how retail investors are positioning themselves for the future.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Sustainable Green Team announces debt reduction initiative
data.description:
• Sustainable Green Team (OTCPK:SGTM) announced on Thursday a restructuring plan aimed at reducing debt and enhancing shareholder value. 
  • As a result of negotiations with significant creditors, the company has established settlements with payment plans that will, over time, reduce our debt by over $25 million. The company said it has already achieved a $6 million reduction in debt.
  • The company said these settlements have also enabled the rescinding of 23 million shares of common stock to the treasury, linked to the reduction of associated debt. This step is crucial in SGTM's strategy to enhance market liquidity, providing shareholders with improved flexibility and increased potential for returns.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Citi sees softer chemical outlook, upgrades Celanese, cuts Olin
data.description:
Analysts at Citibank are bracing for a weaker-than-expected finish to 2025 in the North American commodity chemicals sector, citing sluggish demand growth and heavy inventory overhang, but they still see selective opportunities among individual players.

In a note to clients on Thursday, Citi analyst Patrick Cunningham cut third- and fourth-quarter estimates of earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization across several producers by 5% to 12%, pointing to disappointing polyethylene (PE) demand and surplus supply after strong summer operating rates. Citi warned that companies are likely to pursue conservative cost and working-capital management, setting up a softer exit rate into 2026.

The firm downgraded Olin Corp. (NYSE:OLN) to Neutral/High Risk on concerns over further erosion in ECU margins and weaker chlorine derivative demand. Citi also flagged challenges in epoxy markets, with demand growth falling short of expectations and performance additives remaining muted.

By contrast, Celanese (NYSE:CE) was upgraded to Buy/High Risk, with Citi highlighting cost relief as China curbs coal production and as auto builds and potential divestitures offer upside.

“We believe the risk/reward profile skews to the upside at current valuations,” Citi wrote, assigning a target price of $153.

Elsewhere, Dow (NYSE:DOW) and LyondellBasell (NYSE:LYB) saw 3Q ebitda estimates lowered by about 5%, while Westlake (NYSE:WLK) was trimmed by 3% amid weak operating rates. Huntsman (NYSE:HUN) estimates were cut by 11% as sluggish construction activity weighed on margins, and Eastman Chemical (NYSE:EMN) faced a 2–3% reduction.

Looking ahead, Citi cautioned that expectations for a seasonal recovery have not materialized, leaving the sector vulnerable to continued pricing pressure into the fourth quarter.

“Street estimates will continue to see downward revisions,” the note said.

data.title: Cathie Wood purchases the dip, grabs over 500K shares of the sliding DraftKings
data.description:
Cathie Wood’s Ark Invest moved to capitalize on weakness in DraftKings (NASDAQ:DKNG), scooping up more than half a million shares of the sports-betting operator after a selloff. The stock tumbled 5.9% on Wednesday, extending its one-month slide to nearly 26%.

 Ark Invest disclosed that it purchased a total of 511,049 DraftKings shares across three of its actively managed exchange-traded funds. The bulk of the investment came through the flagship ARK Innovation ETF (BATS:ARKK), which added 350,315 shares. Following the purchase, DraftKings represents the fund’s 37th largest holding, carrying a portfolio weighting of 0.95%.

Additional buying occurred in the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (BATS:ARKW), which picked up 103,872 shares. DraftKings now stands as ARKW’s 31st largest holding, weighted at 1.05%. 

The remainder of the position was accumulated by the ARK Fintech Innovation ETF (BATS:ARKF), which acquired 56,862 shares. Within ARKF, DraftKings holds a 1.64% allocation, making it the fund’s 20th largest position. 

Wood’s move underscores Ark Invest’s strategy of leaning into volatility by adding to positions in high-conviction names during periods of market pressure. Despite DraftKings’ recent decline, the company remains a core component across multiple Ark portfolios.

Year-to-date price action: DKNG -5.5%, ARKK +53.1%, ARKW +61.3%, and ARKF +51.7%.

data.title: Novo’s Wegovy and Lilly’s Zepbound recommended in Europe as first-line obesity treatments
data.description:
A leading European medical group announced a new framework for treating obesity on Thursday, recommending Novo’s (NVO) semaglutide and Lilly’s (NYSE:LLY) tirzepatide, marketed as Wegovy and Zepbound, respectively, should be first-line options for obesity and associated complications.

The European Association for the Study of Obesity (EASO) announced the new treatment algorithm in conjunction with a study published in the journal Nature Medicine, which evaluated the impact of different obesity therapies on weight reduction and obesity-related complications.

“Even though there are several options on the market, the reality is that semaglutide and tirzepatide are so effective that they should be the first choice in almost all cases,” said the study’s co-first author, Andreea Ciudin of Vall d’Hebron University Hospital in Barcelona.

The authors designed the framework using the presence or absence of obesity-related complications to determine the treatment option. They added that when a significant weight reduction is required, tirzepatide and semaglutide should be the treatments of choice, and other drugs such as liraglutide marketed by Novo (NVO) as Victoza should be considered when the weight loss target is lower.

“It is the first framework guided by the presence or absence of obesity-related complications, since weight loss is not the only goal of treatment when complications are present,” added Professor Barbara McGowan of Guy’s & St. Thomasʼ Hospital NHS Trust, another co-first author of the study.
